title: Rebuild 101 DVD Quality
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild 1.01 DVD Quality             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Dec 03, 2009 12:01 am So,for the people that do own the 1.01 DVD.How is it?Is it pretty good?Is the darkness problem I've heard that bad?Is it bad enough to make you want to turn it off or is it bearable?Thanks for answering.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 03, 2009 12:21 am I thought it was a bit contrasty. I was more concerned about the graininess. It almost looked like they took their master from a film print, rather than from a digital master.

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Thu Dec 03, 2009 12:48 am I liked it and found it bearable. the dark scenes are not terrible, but if you have a blue ray player then you minus well get 1.11 to get the better exp.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 03, 2009 1:24 am It was great, people will sit and nit-pick everything. Go buy it, its bloody 20 bucks.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Dec 03, 2009 1:41 am That's what I'm gonna do.I wasnt looking for people's opinion's on wheither or not I should get this.I just wanted to see what people thought and if the darkness made it unbearable.I'm getting this because I've been waiting for this to get english dubbed.But,speaking of 1.11.Is that gonna be like a blueray only DVD?Or will it be available in both regular and Blueray?

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Thu Dec 03, 2009 12:16 pm I didn't notice any quality problems myself, I know the contrast issue is there, but until i checked this forum i had no idea there being a problem with it. Seriously it's not noticeable. But buy both 1.01 and 1.11 if you want to. I sure am.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Dec 03, 2009 12:54 pm I initially didn't have a problem with 1.01, but after watching 1.11 I do find that the difference is quite noticeable and I personally would never go back. I'm waiting until 1.11 comes out on Blu-Ray to buy it... but if you want to buy 1.01 I don't think there's anything wrong with that.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Thu Dec 03, 2009 1:15 pm Well of course after i get 1.11 i doubt i'll ever watch 1.01 again due to 1.11 being obviously superior.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 03, 2009 1:45 pm Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: It was great, people will sit and nit-pick everything. Go buy it, its bloody 20 bucks.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelion » Thu Dec 03, 2009 4:00 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: It was great, people will sit and nit-pick everything. Go buy it, its bloody 20 bucks.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 03, 2009 4:05 pm Anybody here feel the slipcase is totally useless, but can't bring themselves to throw it away, nor can they allow it to become scratched or damaged?

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Thu Dec 03, 2009 4:17 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Anybody here feel the slipcase is totally useless, but can't bring themselves to throw it away, nor can they allow it to become scratched or damaged?

[]:             Quote Post by Pcnerdjack14 » Thu Dec 03, 2009 10:19 pm I had a bit of problems with the colors, but 1.01 still looks pretty good. I keep the slip cases for that reason, they're shinny.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu Dec 03, 2009 10:33 pm when I watched it on my TV, it was pretty dark (Quality-wise), but when I showed it to my bro & sis when I went up, it wasn't as dark on my bro's TV i'm still getting 1.11, regardless

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 05, 2009 11:29 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Anybody here feel the slipcase is totally useless, but can't bring themselves to throw it away, nor can they allow it to become scratched or damaged?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 06, 2009 7:05 pm Question about something: Do you think there was added brightness to the 1.01 Funi DVD? It seemed somewhat lighter than from what I remember of the 1.01 Japanese rip from sometime back. There was some kind of contrast and lightness going on, because Shinji at least looked tan. For reference, here's a scene of him from my Japanese 1.01 rip (from the Japanese release): EDIT: From Logan's non-calibrated TV: I can't rip DVDs on my computer, if somebody could rip the same image of him from the Funimation DVD for comparison purposes, that'd be great. ...Yeeeah, his coloring seemed different in the Funi release. I don't necessarily mind the change that the scenes were brighter compared to the Japanese release, it's just somewhat...odd that was changed to begin with, that's all. EDIT(true)2: Although both images may look the same here, I noticed a huge difference in image quality/brightness comparing my 1.11 Funimation DVD to the Japanese release, at any rate.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Dec 06, 2009 7:18 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: ...Yeeeah, his coloring seemed different in the Funi release. I don't necessarily mind the change that the scenes were brighter compared to the Japanese release, it's just somewhat...odd that was changed to begin with, that's all.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 06, 2009 7:28 pm For those who don't see my end notes above: Although both images I linked to may look the same, I noticed a huge difference in image quality/brightness comparing my 1.01 Funimation DVD (played on my TV) to the Japanese release (played on my computer), at any rate. There is something different about Shinji's skin tone (among other things; the final scene of Shinji and Rei in her plug is no where near as dark as I remember it being with the original Japanese 1.01 release) in Funi 1.01, but yeah, it's possible Funimation took it upon themselves to fix it to avoid any complaints. That'd be awesome if somebody could do their own image comparisons just so we can know if the problems were rectified by Funimation or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Dec 08, 2009 2:01 am I don't care what anyone says; I just don't trust DVD anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Dec 08, 2009 3:19 am While I predominantly watch movies blown up large off a projector in glorious high-def, once Apple is finally able to play BluRays on their computers I will make the full switch.
summary:
[]: Rebuild 1.01 DVD Quality             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Dec 03, 2009 12:01 am So,for the people that do own the 1.01 DVD.How is it?Is it pretty good?Is the darkness problem I've heard that bad?Is it bad enough to make you want to turn it off or is it bearable?Thanks for answering.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 03, 2009 12:21 am I thought it was
